geo_events.title: US-Iran War & Strait of Hormuz Crisis
AI Trade Boom Amid Supply Chain Reshuffling

synthesis_headline: Energy shock collides with AI trade boom as stagflation forces dominate reflation impulses
synthesis_strategy: Maintain energy overweight as the dominant macro force while keeping powder dry for tech re-entry if geopolitical tensions ease. Gold provides hedge against both stagflation and potential policy errors. Avoid duration risk given Fed's impossible position.
bull_trigger: Brent crude falls below $100 and stays there for two weeks
bear_trigger: European TTF gas prices break above €75/MWh or US gasoline hits $6/gallon

geo_summary: March 2026 presents a classic stagflationary environment with simultaneous supply shocks driving inflation while hawkish central bank policy constrains growth. Geopolitical tensions from Iran conflict

geo_events.title: Federal Reserve Hawks Maintain Higher Rates

synthesis_headline: Stagflation playbook in full effect as geopolitical risk premium drives energy while Fed maintains hawkish stance despite growth concerns.
synthesis_strategy: Overweight the stagflation trade that's already working - energy and real assets that benefit from supply disruptions and persistent inflation. Underweight duration and growth stocks until geopolitical risks subside or Fed pivots. The regime change is clear but timing the exit remains impossible.
bull_trigger: Diplomatic breakthrough on Iran or oil inventory release driving WTI below $85
bear_trigger: Iran blocks Hormuz shipping lanes or attacks Saudi facilities
